page: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, delivering 24 physical cores and 24 vCPUs at a clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8azn.6xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across several compute and memory metrics. Single-core performance is particularly strong, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores ranking in the top 10%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks, both read and write, also reaches top-tier status, supported by the fast DDR5 memory generation. The processor's instruction set, which includes AVX-512 and cryptography extensions like AES and SHA_NI, contributes to strong multi-core execution and top-tier database operations in Passmark testing.

The server exhibits excellent efficiency for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance and fast memory throughput without the need for local NVMe drives. It is highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference, where its top-tier text generation and prompt processing capabilities for small to medium models can be fully utilized.
description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server powered by a dedicated 24-core AMD EPYC 9R05 processor running at 5.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU performance, high large-block memory bandwidth, and excellent database operation speeds. It is optimized for workloads such as database management, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring a 24-core AMD EPYC processor at 5.0 GHz, 96 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for high-performance database and compute workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8azn.6xlarge is a general-purpose server with a 24-core AMD EPYC CPU, 96 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-frequency general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, rapid DDR5 memory, and high-speed baseline network connectivity.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 24 cores running at 5.0 GHz
96.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization
Top-tier single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
Well-suited for database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
